emails.subject: Wedding guest block rate
emails.content: Hello, I'm attending a wedding at your hotel next month and the bride mentioned there's a group rate. Can you send me the booking link for the Wilson-Stevens wedding block? Thank you!

emails.subject: Beach towels and chairs?
emails.content: Quick question - do you provide beach towels and chairs for guests? We're staying APH-2416 and planning to spend time at the beach. Thanks!

emails.subject: Spa services and pricing
emails.content: Do you have a spa? I'm staying APH-2406 and would love to book a massage. Can you send me the spa menu and pricing? Also, what are the hours? Thanks!

emails.subject: Iron and ironing board in room?
emails.content: Quick question - do your rooms come with an iron and ironing board? I have important meetings during my stay (APH-2405) and need to look presentable. If not, is there laundry service?

emails.subject: Birthday surprise for husband
emails.content: Hi! Checking in tomorrow for APH-2423. It's my husband's 50th birthday and I'd love to surprise him. Can you help arrange a birthday cake to be delivered to our room? Happy to pay extra. Thank you!

emails.subject: Concierge services available?
emails.content: Do you have concierge services? I'm staying APH-2418 and need help booking a dinner reservation at Nobu Malibu for Saturday night. Also interested in recommendations for activities. Thanks!

emails.subject: Room cleaning preferences
emails.content: Hello! I'm in room for APH-2443 and prefer not to have daily housekeeping. Can you please skip cleaning my room during my stay? I'll request fresh towels if needed. Thanks for helping reduce environmental impact!

emails.subject: Temperature control in room
emails.content: The AC in my room (APH-2421) doesn't seem to be working properly. It's very warm and I can't get it below 74 degrees. Can maintenance check it? Room temperature is really important for my sleep. Thank you!

emails.subject: Noise complaint - room 205
emails.content: I'm in room for APH-2444 and there's been loud music coming from room 205 for the past hour. It's now 11:30 PM. Can someone please address this? I have an early morning meeting. Thank you.

emails.subject: Room change request
emails.content: I'm checking in today (APH-2445) and just realized I booked an ocean view but would actually prefer a quieter city view room on a higher floor if available. Can we swap? What would be the price difference?

emails.subject: Pet policy question
emails.content: Quick question - do you allow pets? I have a small dog (15 lbs) and would love to bring him for my stay March 1-5 (APH-2430). If yes, what's the pet fee? If not, can you recommend a nearby pet-friendly hotel? Thanks!

emails.subject: Dietary restrictions for breakfast
emails.content: Hi! We're checking in tomorrow (confirmation APH-2446). My husband has a severe gluten allergy. Do you have gluten-free options for breakfast? Also need to avoid cross-contamination. Thanks!

emails.subject: Loyalty points not showing
emails.content: I'm a Gold member and just checked in yesterday (APH-2444) but I don't see the points credited to my account yet. Also, I should be getting complimentary breakfast as a Gold member but the front desk said that's not included. Can you clarify your loyalty benefits?

emails.subject: Need to extend my stay
emails.content: Hi there, I'm currently checked in (APH-2416) and need to extend my checkout from March 4th to March 6th. Is my Junior Suite available for those extra nights? Let me know the rate. Thanks!

emails.subject: Business center and printing
emails.content: I'm checked in currently (APH-2436) and need to print some documents for a meeting. Do you have a business center? What are the hours? Is there a charge for printing? Thanks!

emails.subject: Never received confirmation email
emails.content: I booked a room through Booking.com but never received a confirmation email. My booking reference is APH-2442. Can you resend it? I'm worried my reservation didn't go through properly. I'm currently checked in but want the confirmation for my records.

emails.subject: Anniversary flowers delivered?
emails.content: Hi! I had a bouquet of red roses delivered to room for APH-2439 yesterday but my wife says they never arrived. Can you check if they were delivered? Order was from ProFlowers. Thank you!

emails.subject: Need to cancel - full refund expected
emails.content: I need to cancel my current reservation APH-2431 and check out early due to a family emergency. I know I'm already checked in but this is urgent. I booked this 2 weeks ago and expect a refund for the remaining nights. Can you process this today?

emails.subject: Extra pillows please
emails.content: Could I get 2 extra pillows delivered to my room? I'm in APH-2414 and the pillows provided are too soft for me. Firm pillows preferred. Thanks so much!

emails.subject: Checkout time confirmation
emails.content: Hi, I'm currently checked in (APH-2448) and checking out on March 7. Just want to confirm checkout is at 11 AM. Is late checkout available? Would love to stay until 1 PM if possible. Thank you!

emails.subject: Room key not working
emails.content: My room key for APH-2432 has stopped working - I've tried it 5 times and the door won't open. I'm standing outside my room now. Can you reactivate it or should I come to the front desk? Thanks!

emails.subject: Coffee maker not working
emails.content: Good morning! The coffee maker in my room (APH-2451) isn't working. I really need my morning coffee! Can you send someone to fix it or bring a new one? Thanks!

emails.subject: Mini bar charges
emails.content: I'm checked in currently (APH-2453) and noticed the mini bar in my room. Are these automatically charged to the room? What are the prices? Just want to know before I use anything. Thanks!

emails.subject: Lost item - black laptop charger
emails.content: Hi, I checked out yesterday from APH-2454 and realized I left my black MacBook Pro charger in the room. Has it been turned in to lost and found? Can you ship it to me if found? Happy to pay shipping. Thanks!

emails.subject: Taxi service to LAX
emails.content: Hi, I'm checking out tomorrow morning at 6 AM (APH-2502) and need a reliable taxi to LAX. Can you arrange this? My flight is at 9 AM so I need to leave by 6:15 AM. Thank you!

emails.subject: [DRAFT] Pushback — HALO 3C smart sensors purchase (security & necessity concern)
emails.content:
[DRAFT — needs review and fact-check before sending]

Subject: Pushback request — HALO 3C smart sensors purchase (security & necessity concern)

Team,

I want to raise concerns before we approve any further purchase of the HALO 3C "smart sensors" being proposed as bathroom and common-area smoke/vape detectors. Two issues: (1) the actual security track record of these devices, and (2) whether they map to any environmental safety requirement we are actually obligated to meet.

1) Security disclosure — DEF CON 33 (August 2025)
A talk titled "Unmasking the Snitch Puck: the creepy IoT surveillance tech in the school bathroom" was given at DEF CON 33 by researchers Reynaldo Vasquez-Garcia and "Nyx" on August 8, 2025. They publicly demonstrated full remote takeover of the HALO 3C (made by IPVideo Corporation, a Motorola Solutions subsidiary — the same vendor behind SentryVMS, which we already use indoors).

Reference video (DEF CON official upload):
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WCnojaEpF2I

Vendor security advisory (confirms vulnerability and patch):
https://halodetect.com/product-security/security-advisory/

Reported by Wired / 404 Media:
https://www.wired.com/story/school-bathroom-vape-detector-audio-bug/

Affected firmware (per vendor advisory):
 - HALO 3C running 2.15.0.13 or lower
 - HALO 2/2C running 2.14.3.0 or lower
 - Fix: firmware 2.17.0.2

Key findings the researchers demonstrated:
 - Hardware: device is essentially a Raspberry Pi with attached sensors (temperature/humidity, accelerometer, air-quality/gas sensors) — and, notably, microphones plus a speaker. Confirmed by physical teardown.
 - Login brute-force: the device's rate limiting on its web interface was effectively broken; passwords could be guessed at roughly 3,000 attempts per minute.
 - Unsigned/poorly-protected firmware updates: the encryption key required to sign custom firmware was shipped inside the publicly downloadable firmware package itself — described as "a locked box with the key taped to the underside."
 - Result: any attacker on the same network could install custom firmware, disable detection, fabricate vape/gunshot alerts, play arbitrary audio out of the device's speaker, and turn the on-board microphones into a real-time eavesdropping bug.

Note on operating system / kernel: I have not been able to confirm an exact Linux kernel version from public sources (the DEF CON deck is mostly screenshots and the researchers did not publish a CVE list). What is public and confirmed is the Raspberry Pi class hardware and a network-exposed Linux-based firmware. We should ask IPVideo directly for: (a) exact kernel version shipped on the units we'd receive, (b) CVE IDs covered by 2.17.0.2, (c) whether secure boot / signed firmware is now enforced, (d) microphone hardware kill-switch status. [FACT-CHECK: confirm these specifics before sending.]

2) Why this matters for us as a hotel
 - We would be installing networked microphones in bathrooms and "open spaces" — areas where guests have a strong expectation of privacy. Even with the patch, the researchers' point stands: the hardware itself is surveillance-capable, and "no software patch makes a microphone not a microphone."
 - We are not a school or correctional facility. I am not aware of any environmental safety regulation we are bound by that requires THC/vape detection, aggression detection, gunshot detection, or spoken-keyword detection in guest areas. A standard listed smoke detector (UL 217 / NFPA 72) is what fire code and our insurer require. If there is a specific regulation driving this purchase, please cite the exact code section so we can evaluate.
 - Liability exposure: putting always-on microphones in bathrooms creates a privacy/wiretap-law footprint we currently do not have. A single guest complaint or a compromised device making the news is a reputational event we do not want.
 - Vendor concentration: we already rely on SentryVMS (same parent: IPVideo / Motorola Solutions) for indoor video. Putting our smoke detection on the same vendor stack increases blast radius if a future SentryVMS or HALO vulnerability gets weaponized inside our network.
 - Budget framing: these are not "good for our budget" once you factor in unit cost (multiples of a code-compliant smoke detector), recurring cloud/management costs, network segmentation work, and the ongoing burden of tracking firmware advisories. The cheaper, lower-risk path is standard listed smoke/CO detectors plus, where we genuinely need vape detection (e.g., enforcement of non-smoking rooms), a non-networked or audio-free model.

Proposed next step before we commit
Hold the purchase until IT can answer, in writing:
 - Which exact model and firmware version we'd be receiving today.
 - Confirmation that microphone capture is disabled in firmware AND that the device will be on an isolated VLAN with no inbound paths from guest Wi-Fi.
 - The specific environmental/safety requirement this purchase is intended to satisfy, with a code citation.
 - A vendor statement on the DEF CON 33 findings and the remediation status.

Happy to discuss. I am not against better detection — I am against installing networked microphones in guest bathrooms without a clear regulatory reason and a clear security posture.

— [Your name]

[End draft — review, edit, and double-check the technical claims above (especially the kernel/CVE line) before sending.]
